SUMMARY: NESTED | FIELD | CONSTR | METHOD DETAIL: FIELD | CONSTR | METHOD

javax.media.mscontrol.mixer
Interface MixerAdapter

All Superinterfaces:
AllocationEventNotifier, Joinable, JoinableContainer, JoinEventNotifier, MediaObject, ResourceContainer, java.io.Serializable
public interface MixerAdapter
extends JoinableContainer, ResourceContainer

A MixerAdapter is the materialization of a "Mixer port".
Additionally, it can include media processing resources.
(The resources cannot be players or recorders).
In most cases, the application will want a dtmf clamper, and/or a volume control.
This is also the place to set a VideoLayout per mixer port.
The default behavior is to let all streams pass through, in both directions, without modification.
This object is built with createMixerAdapter in its different forms..
It should be released by the application (However the Mixer or the MediaSession will clean it up on exit).

static Configuration<MixerAdapter> DTMF_CLAMP
          This config clamps the DTMF's that would otherwise enter the Mixer.

          This config clamps the DTMF's that would otherwise enter the Mixer, and include a volume control.

          This config is a pass-through, no media processing is performed on any stream.

Field Detail

EMPTY

static final Configuration<MixerAdapter> EMPTY
This config is a pass-through, no media processing is performed on any stream.
A MixerAdapter with this config is suitable for designating a specific Mixer "port", and/or assigning a container Id to it.

DTMF_CLAMP

static final Configuration<MixerAdapter> DTMF_CLAMP
This config clamps the DTMF's that would otherwise enter the Mixer.
The DTMF clamper is always active, and has no interface at all.

DTMFCLAMP_VOLUME

static final Configuration<MixerAdapter> DTMFCLAMP_VOLUME
This config clamps the DTMF's that would otherwise enter the Mixer, and include a volume control.
See VolumeConstants.
